questions.question: Design a 4-bit full adder using basic gates. What is the name of the basic gate used as an inverter in the carry output?
questions.explanation: The NOT gate (D) is used as an inverter in the carry output because it produces a complement of the input

questions.question: A 4-bit full adder requires 6 basic gates. What type of gate is typically used as an AND gate in the carry output?
questions.explanation: The AND gate (C) is typically used as an AND gate in the carry output because it produces an AND operation between two inputs

questions.question: In a 4-bit full adder, the sum output is obtained by applying basic gates to the inputs A, B, and Cin. Which basic gate is used to perform the sum operation?
questions.explanation: The XOR gate (C) is used to perform the sum operation because it produces an XOR operation between the inputs A and B

questions.question: A 4-bit subtractor is similar to a 4-bit full adder. What type of basic gate is used as an inverter in the borrow output?
questions.explanation: The NOT gate (D) is used as an inverter in the borrow output because it produces a complement of the input

questions.question: A 4-bit full adder and subtractor have the same basic gate structure. What is the primary difference between the two?
options: B) Type of basic gate used
questions.explanation: The primary difference between the two is the functionality of the gates, as an adder performs addition and a subtractor performs subtraction
